relationship-dense.content:
# FACEBOOK CONTENT PLAYBOOK
## Second Act Dating — 20 Posts

---

### Post 1 — VALUE POST
**Funnel Stage:** TOF  
**Retargeting:** Y — Target divorced men 45+ who've engaged with dating content

The women you attract are a mirror of your emotional state.

If you're attracting unavailable women, drama queens, or women who remind you of your ex — look in the mirror.

Here's what's really happening:

You're dating from the wound of your divorce instead of from wholeness. When you carry unhealed attachment trauma, you unconsciously seek partners who validate your wounded identity.

Angry men attract angry women.
Desperate men attract unavailable women.
Wounded men attract broken women.

Try this today: Before your next date, ask yourself "Am I showing up to heal my wounds or to find a partner?"

The right woman doesn't want to fix you. She wants to choose you.

What patterns have you noticed in the women you attract?

---

### Post 2 — STORY POST
**Funnel Stage:** MOF  
**Retargeting:** Y — Target men who've viewed program content

After my divorce in 2014, I was making $180K in my therapy practice but sleeping on a mattress on the floor of a rental.

I spent 18 months dating badly. Angry, distrustful, attracting women who reminded me of my ex every single time.

I tried every dating app, read every book, went to therapy. Nothing worked.

Then my mentor said something that broke me open:

"You're not dating to find a partner. You're dating to prove your ex wrong. That's why every relationship fails."

He was right. I was dating from the wound of my divorce, not from wholeness.

That conversation changed everything. I did 2 years of my own therapy, closed the practice, and launched Second Act Dating specifically for men coming out of marriage.

Today I have a 3000-person community, run 6 retreats per year, and I'm remarried to a woman I met in my own program community.

The breakthrough wasn't finding the right woman. It was becoming the right man.

---

### Post 3 — BELIEF-SHIFTING POST
**Funnel Stage:** MOF  
**Retargeting:** Y — Target men researching dating coaches

You wouldn't try to fix a leaky roof by buying better rain gear.

You'd fix the roof itself.

Dating apps and strategies are rain gear — they might keep you dry temporarily, but they don't fix the leak.

Your attachment patterns from your divorce are the leak.

Until you fix that, you'll keep getting soaked no matter how much gear you buy.

Most divorced men think "I just need better matches and the right woman will appear."

The truth: The Second Act Method — a 3-phase Gottman-based protocol for rebuilding attachment capacity after divorce — is the proven path to attracting the right partner.

Dr. John Gottman's research shows that successful relationships depend on attachment security, not dating strategies.

When you understand that your internal state determines the quality of women you attract, you can find a loving relationship and become The Emotionally Intelligent Second-Act Man.

Stop buying rain gear. Fix the roof.

---

### Post 4 — RESULTS/PROOF POST
**Funnel Stage:** BOF  
**Retargeting:** Y — Target warm leads and discovery call no-shows

Dr. James K. was a cardiac surgeon.

Divorced 3 years. Zero second dates in 18 months.

He had tried every dating app, paid for premium features, even hired a photographer for better profile pics.

Nothing worked.

He was still The Wounded-Heart Repeater — dating from his divorce wound instead of from wholeness.

After 6 months in The Second Act Method, he became The Emotionally Intelligent Second-Act Man.

Result: Engaged to a woman he met at our November retreat in 8 months.

As he told me: "The only reason I'm not alone at 52 is this program."

The difference wasn't his photos or his profile.

The difference was that he stopped dating from wound and started dating from wholeness.

When you heal your attachment patterns, you naturally attract women who are looking for what you're offering instead of what you're trying to escape.

---

### Post 5 — CTA POST
**Funnel Stage:** BOF  
**Retargeting:** N — Organic reach only

There are two types of divorced men over 45:

Those who keep making the same dating mistakes for years.

And those who invest in proven frameworks that work.

The Second Act Method isn't for everyone.

It's for divorced men 45-60 who make $200K+, have been single for 1-4 years, and are tired of dating apps that lead nowhere.

Men who are ready to stop dating from the wound and start dating from wholeness.

Men who want to become The Emotionally Intelligent Second-Act Man — a guy who attracts the right women because he finally knows who he is.

If that's you, DM me "SECOND ACT" and I'll send you the details about our next group.

If you're looking for quick fixes or pickup artist tactics, this isn't for you.

But if you're ready to do the inner work that creates lasting love, let's talk.

---

### Post 6 — VALUE POST
**Funnel Stage:** TOF  
**Retargeting:** Y — Target men researching post-divorce dating

Every woman who ghosts you is showing you exactly what you're unconsciously communicating.

Desperation shows up in your texts.
Neediness shows up in your conversation.
Emotional unavailability shows up in your energy.

Women can sense when you're dating from wound instead of wholeness.

Here's what to track after every date:

1. Did I show up to perform or to connect?
2. Was I seeking validation or offering value?
3. Did I feel triggered by anything she said or did?

Try this: Before your next date, spend 10 minutes getting grounded in who you are and what you bring to a relationship.

The right woman doesn't want your performance. She wants your presence.

What's the biggest difference you've noticed between your best and worst dates?

---

### Post 7 — STORY POST
**Funnel Stage:** MOF  
**Retargeting:** Y — Target engaged audience

I was 38 years old, making $180K, and still sleeping on a mattress on the floor.

Not because I couldn't afford a bed frame.

Because I was so emotionally shut down after my divorce that I couldn't even invest in basic comfort for myself.

I was dating from the wound, attracting women who reminded me of my ex, and wondering why every relationship felt like déjà vu.

My mentor's words hit like a sledgehammer:

"You're not dating to find a partner. You're dating to prove your ex wrong."

That's when I realized I was unconsciously seeking women who would validate my negative beliefs about relationships.

I was looking for proof that I was unlovable, not for someone who could love me.

Two years of my own therapy later, I launched The Second Act Method.

Because my father never came back for me, and I refuse to let another man disappear from his own life.

Every man deserves to be chosen, not settled for.

---

### Post 8 — BELIEF-SHIFTING POST
**Funnel Stage:** MOF  
**Retargeting:** Y — Target men who've viewed pricing content

$9,500 over 6 months is $52 per day.

Less than what you spend on lunch while eating alone.

If you can't find $9,500 to invest in your happiness, you're proving you don't believe you deserve love.

The brutal truth: You're already spending $400-600 monthly on dating apps, premium subscriptions, and therapy that doesn't address your dating patterns.

That's [COACH: Insert your real dollar amount here]-7,200 yearly with no results.

The Second Act Method is an investment in your life quality that pays for itself through finding the right partner.

The ROI on relationship capacity is infinite.

You have to spend money to make love.

The question isn't whether you can afford to invest in The Second Act Method.

The question is whether you can afford another year of waking up alone.

---

### Post 9 — VALUE POST
**Funnel Stage:** TOF  
**Retargeting:** Y — Target divorced men interested in dating advice

"I'll start dating seriously when work slows down."

Translation: "I'm scared of being vulnerable again."

Work will expand to fill whatever time you give it.

But your window for finding lasting love is finite.

At 45+, you can't afford to waste years hoping things will magically improve.

Here's what to do instead:

Block time for your emotional growth like you would any important business meeting.

Prioritize your relationship capacity the same way you prioritize your income growth.

Warren Buffett said the most important decision you'll make is who you marry, because it affects everything else in your life.

If the most successful investor in history prioritizes partnership over portfolio optimization, maybe it's time to examine where you're putting your energy.

What would change in your life if you treated finding love with the same urgency as hitting your income goals?

---

### Post 10 — RESULTS/PROOF POST
**Funnel Stage:** BOF  
**Retargeting:** Y — Target men who've engaged with program content

Carlos M. was drinking too much and dating women 15 years younger.

Two years divorced. Running from himself instead of healing.

He thought his problem was finding age-appropriate women.

His real problem: dating from the wound of his divorce, seeking validation instead of connection.

After 6 months in The Second Act Method plus 1 retreat:

Sober for 10 months.
Dating an age-appropriate partner for 9 months and counting.

As he told me: "I stopped running from myself."

The transformation wasn't about finding different women.

It was about becoming a different man.

When you heal your attachment patterns, you naturally attract partners who match your new emotional state.

Wounded attracts wounded. Healed attracts healed.

Which are you?

---

### Post 11 — IDENTITY POST
**Funnel Stage:** MOF  
**Retargeting:** Y — Target men who've viewed identity content

There are two types of divorced men over 45:

**The Wounded-Heart Repeater:**
Sends 30 messages a week on dating apps.
Gets 2 first dates a month that go nowhere.
Feels invisible, angry, defeated.
Wakes up to an empty bed every morning.
Keeps attracting women who remind him of his ex.

**The Emotionally Intelligent Second-Act Man:**
Attracts quality women because he knows who he is.
Gets 3 meaningful dates per month with right-fit women.
Feels hopeful, selective, confident.
Sleeps peacefully knowing he's on the right path.
Chooses partners instead of settling for whoever's available.

You're either becoming The Emotionally Intelligent Second-Act Man or staying The Wounded-Heart Repeater.

There's no middle ground.

The choice is yours.

But you can't be both.

Which man do you choose to become?

---

### Post 12 — BELIEF-SHIFTING POST
**Funnel Stage:** MOF  
**Retargeting:** Y — Target men researching dating programs

"Dating coaches are for 25-year-olds."

Post-divorce dating at 45+ is emotional heart surgery.

You wouldn't use a general contractor to perform heart surgery — you'd want a cardiac specialist who understands your specific condition.

The Second Act Method isn't generic dating advice designed for men who've never been married.

It's specialized frameworks for men rebuilding after divorce.

Most dating advice assumes you have a clean emotional slate.

But you don't. You have attachment wounds, trust issues, and patterns from your marriage that need specific healing.

Generic advice like "just be yourself" doesn't work when yourself is wounded, reactive, and carrying divorce trauma.

You need age-appropriate, trauma-informed approaches that address the root cause.

That's why The Second Act Method is based on 40+ years of Gottman research, not pickup artist theories.

You already know this in business — you don't use generic strategies for industry-specific problems.

Apply the same logic to finding love.

---

### Post 13 — STORY POST
**Funnel Stage:** MOF  
**Retargeting:** Y — Target warm audience

The moment everything changed for me:

"You're not dating to find a partner. You're dating to prove your ex wrong. That's why every relationship fails."

My mentor's words broke me open.

He was right.

I was unconsciously seeking women who would validate my wounded identity. Looking for proof that I was unlovable instead of someone who could love me.

Every failed date was evidence that my ex was right about me.

Every woman who ghosted me confirmed my negative beliefs about relationships.

I wasn't dating from hope. I was dating from hurt.

That realization led me to develop The Second Act Method:

Phase 1: Grounding — heal the wound
Phase 2: Rebuilding — build new patterns  
Phase 3: Attracting — choose from wholeness

Today I'm remarried to a woman I met in my own program community.

Not because I found the right woman, but because I became the right man.

The breakthrough isn't finding someone who accepts your wounds.

It's healing the wounds so you can attract wholeness.

---

### Post 14 — VALUE POST
**Funnel Stage:** TOF  
**Retargeting:** Y — Target men interested in relationship advice

You don't have a dating app problem.

You have a wounded attachment problem.

Surface-level solutions (better photos, more matches) don't address post-divorce attachment wounds.

Here's what's really happening:

Your divorce created an attachment wound that makes you unconsciously seek partners who trigger your unhealed trauma.

That's why you keep attracting women who remind you of your ex.
That's why every relationship feels like déjà vu.
That's why you get triggered by the same behaviors over and over.

Try this self-assessment:

1. Do I get defensive when women ask about my divorce?
2. Do I compare every woman to my ex?
3. Do I feel like I'm performing instead of being authentic?

If you answered yes to any of these, you're dating from wound, not wholeness.

The Second Act Method addresses the root cause so you naturally attract women who are looking for what you're offering instead of what you're trying to escape.

What patterns have you noticed in your post-divorce dating life?

---

### Post 15 — CTA POST
**Funnel Stage:** BOF  
**Retargeting:** N — Organic reach only

I only work with 40 men at a time in The Second Act Method.

Here's why:

Real transformation requires personal attention, not mass-market programs.

You get weekly group coaching calls where we address your specific triggers.
Monthly 1-on-1 sessions where we customize frameworks for your situation.
24/7 access to our Brotherhood community of 3,000 men who understand your journey.

This isn't a course you complete alone.

It's a 6-month transformation with expert guidance and peer support.

But it's not for everyone.

**This is for you if:**
- You're divorced, 45-60, making $200K+
- You've been single 1-4 years with no real traction
- You're tired of first dates that go nowhere
- You're ready to stop dating from your wound

**This is NOT for you if:**
- You think the problem is just bad luck with apps
- You want quick fixes for deep problems
- You're not ready to examine your own patterns

If you're ready to become The Emotionally Intelligent Second-Act Man, DM me "DISCOVERY" for details about our next group.

---

### Post 16 — RESULTS/PROOF POST
**Funnel Stage:** BOF  
**Retargeting:** Y — Target men who've viewed testimonials

Mike D. avoided dating entirely for 18 months after his divorce.

Tech founder. Successful business. Completely shut down emotionally.

He thought he could figure it out himself when he was "ready."

But ready never came.

After the full Second Act Method program plus 2 retreats:

In a committed relationship with someone he calls "the right person at the right time."

Timeline: 11 months from avoidance to love.

As he told me: "I wasn't broken. I was healing."

The difference wasn't that he found someone different.

The difference was that he became someone different.

When you do the inner work that most divorced men skip, you naturally attract women who've done their work too.

Healed attracts healed.

The question isn't whether you're broken.

The question is whether you're willing to heal.

---

### Post 17 — POLARIZING/FILTER POST
**Funnel Stage:** MOF  
**Retargeting:** N — Organic reach only

Unpopular opinion:

If you're not willing to invest in your relationship capacity, you don't deserve the relationship you want.

I said what I said.

You'll spend $400-600 monthly on dating apps for years but won't invest in the system that makes apps unnecessary.

You'll pay for premium features, professional photos, and dating advice that doesn't work.

But when someone offers you proven frameworks that have helped 420 men find lasting love, suddenly you're "not ready" or "it's too expensive."

The truth: You're not ready for love if you're not ready to invest in becoming lovable.

The right woman doesn't want a man who's cheap about his own growth.

She wants a man who invests in becoming his best self.

If you disagree, that's fine.

This isn't for you.

But if you're tired of making excuses and ready to make changes, DM me "READY" and let's talk.

---

### Post 18 — BELIEF-SHIFTING POST
**Funnel Stage:** MOF  
**Retargeting:** Y — Target men who've viewed method content

You've been trying to heal your attachment wounds alone for years.

How's that working?

Successful men hire experts in every area of their lives:

Business coaches for revenue growth.
Financial advisors for wealth building.
Personal trainers for fitness goals.

But when it comes to finding love, suddenly you think you should figure it out yourself.

The fastest path to any goal is learning from someone who's already achieved it.

I rebuilt my own relationship capacity after divorce and helped 420 men do the same.

My frameworks can save you years of trial and error while giving you the accountability and community you need to break your patterns.

What takes 3 years alone takes 6 months with expert guidance.

Time is your most valuable asset.

You need a coach, not another course.

Transformation requires relationship, not just information.

The question isn't whether you need help.

The question is whether you're ready to accept it.

---

### Post 19 — VALUE POST
**Funnel Stage:** TOF  
**Retargeting:** Y — Target men interested in dating psychology

The brutal truth about why you're still single after 3 years of trying:

You think you need better matches.

You actually need better attachment patterns.

Here's what's really happening:

You're unconsciously attracted to women who trigger your unhealed wounds so you can heal them.

This is why divorced men often find themselves in relationships that feel eerily similar to their marriage.

You're being drawn to the same patterns until you do the inner work to break the cycle.

Quick self-check:

1. Do the women you attract have similar traits to your ex?
2. Do you find yourself in the same arguments with different women?
3. Do you feel like you're dating the same person in different bodies?

If yes, you're dating from wound, not wholeness.

The solution isn't finding different women.

The solution is becoming a different man.

When you heal your attachment patterns, you naturally attract partners who match your new emotional state.

What patterns have you noticed in your dating life?

---

### Post 20 — CTA POST
**Funnel Stage:** BOF  
**Retargeting:** Y — Target men who've engaged with multiple posts

Today you choose:

Another year of empty beds and failed first dates.

Or 6 months of proven transformation.

I've helped 420 divorced men find lasting love using The Second Act Method.

63 out of 80 clients achieved meaningful relationship results.

Dr. James K.: 0 second dates in 18 months to engaged in 8 months.
Carlos M.: Drinking and dating 20-somethings to sober and committed for 9 months.
Mike D.: Complete avoidance to "the right person at the right time" in 11 months.

The difference wasn't that they found better women.

The difference was that they became better men.

They stopped being The Wounded-Heart Repeater and became The Emotionally Intelligent Second-Act Man.

The choice is yours.

But I can only show you the door. You're the one who has to walk through it.

If you're ready to stop dating from your wound and start dating from your wholeness, book a discovery call.

Link in bio.

This opportunity won't be around forever.

---

**CONTENT CALENDAR SUMMARY:**
- 5 Value Posts (TOF awareness)
- 4 Story Posts (MOF connection)
- 4 Belief-Shifting Posts (MOF transformation)
- 3 Results/Proof Posts (BOF social proof)
- 3 CTA Posts (BOF direct invitation)
- 1 Identity Post (MOF positioning)
- 1 Polarizing/Filter Post (anti-avatar repelling)

**RETARGETING STRATEGY:**
- 16 posts marked for retargeting ads
- Target audiences: divorced men 45+, dating content engagers, program viewers, warm leads
- 4 posts organic only to maintain authentic reach

relationship-dense.content:
# Facebook/Instagram Ad Copy for Second Act Dating

## Ad 1: Problem/Agitation (Cold Audience)

**Primary Text (Short):**
You're successful in business but keep attracting the same type of woman who reminds you of your ex. Sound familiar?

**Primary Text (Long):**
You're successful in business but keep attracting the same type of woman who reminds you of your ex. Sound familiar?

Here's the thing most divorced men over 45 don't realize: You're not dating to find a partner. You're dating to prove your ex wrong.

That's why every relationship feels like déjà vu. That's why you swipe right on women who trigger the same arguments. That's why you're still sleeping alone after 2-4 years of "putting yourself out there."

The dating apps aren't broken. Your dating frame is.

When you date from the wound of your divorce instead of from wholeness, you attract women who are also dating from their wounds. It's wounded energy calling to wounded energy.

I know because I spent 18 months after my divorce doing exactly this. Making $180K as a therapist but sleeping on a mattress on the floor, angry and suspicious of every woman's motives.

Until my mentor broke me open with one sentence that changed everything.

If you're ready to stop repeating the patterns that broke your first marriage, I can show you how to become The Emotionally Intelligent Second-Act Man — a guy who attracts the right women because he finally knows who he is.

**Headline:** Stop Dating Your Ex Over and Over

**Description:** Break the cycle that keeps you single

**CTA Button:** Learn More

**Suggested Image/Video Direction:** Split image showing a frustrated man swiping on phone vs. confident man having dinner with an elegant woman

**Targeting Notes:**
- Audience: Cold (divorced men 45-60, income $200K+)
- Optimization: Landing page views
- Budget: [COACH: Insert your real dollar amount here]-100/day

---

## Ad 2: Social Proof/Case Study (Warm Audience)

**Primary Text (Short):**
Dr. James K. was divorced 3 years with 0 second dates in 18 months. 8 months later, he's engaged.

**Primary Text (Long):**
Dr. James K. was divorced 3 years with 0 second dates in 18 months. 8 months later, he's engaged.

"The only reason I'm not alone at 52 is this program." — Dr. James K., cardiac surgeon

James came to me burned out on dating apps. Successful surgeon, great income, but couldn't get past a first date to save his life. Sound familiar?

The problem wasn't his profile. The problem wasn't his conversation skills. The problem was he was dating from the wound of his divorce instead of from wholeness.

Here's what we did:

**Grounding (4 weeks):** Post-divorce identity work to reset his emotional baseline
**Rebuilding (8 weeks):** Gottman-based attachment pattern work to establish a new dating frame  
**Attracting (12 weeks):** Real-world dating practice with right-fit women

Result? James met his fiancée at one of our quarterly retreats. Not on an app. Not through friends. Through the Brotherhood community of men who've done the work to date from wholeness.

This is The Second Act Method. A 3-phase Gottman-based protocol for rebuilding attachment capacity after divorce.

420 men have been through this program. 63 out of 80 recent graduates are now in committed relationships.

If you're ready to stop dating from your wound and start choosing from wholeness, let's talk.

**Headline:** From 0 Second Dates to Engaged in 8 Months

**Description:** Real results from The Second Act Method

**CTA Button:** Apply Now

**Suggested Image/Video Direction:** Professional headshot of a confident man in his 50s with testimonial quote overlay

**Targeting Notes:**
- Audience: Warm (website visitors, video viewers)
- Optimization: Conversions (applications)
- Budget: $30-50/day

---

## Ad 3: Lead Magnet/SLO (Cold Audience)

**Primary Text (Short):**
Take the Second-Act Dating Audit and discover why your dates never go anywhere.

**Primary Text (Long):**
Take the Second-Act Dating Audit and discover why your dates never go anywhere.

You're successful. You're accomplished. You make good money and have your life together.

So why are you still single after 2-4 years of dating?

The truth? Most divorced men over 45 are dating from the wound of their divorce instead of from wholeness. They're unconsciously attracted to women who trigger the same patterns that broke their first marriage.

That's why you keep matching with women who remind you of your ex. That's why conversations fizzle out. That's why you feel invisible on the apps.

The Second-Act Dating Audit reveals exactly where you're stuck and gives you a clear roadmap to start attracting the right women.

This isn't another dating app strategy guide. This is a Gottman-based assessment that identifies your specific attachment patterns and shows you how to date from wholeness instead of from your wound.

Take the audit now. It's free and takes 5 minutes.

Because the right woman is out there. But she's not looking for a wounded man trying to prove his ex wrong. She's looking for The Emotionally Intelligent Second-Act Man who knows exactly who he is.

**Headline:** Why Your Dates Never Go Anywhere

**Description:** Free 5-minute assessment

**CTA Button:** Download

**Suggested Image/Video Direction:** Clean, professional graphic with "Dating Audit" text and masculine color scheme (navy/gray)

**Targeting Notes:**
- Audience: Cold (lookalike of email list)
- Optimization: Landing page views
- Budget: [COACH: Insert your real dollar amount here]-150/day

---

## Ad 4: Retargeting/Belief-Shift Content (No Hard CTA)

**Primary Text (Short):**
Your dating apps aren't broken. Your dating frame is.

**Primary Text (Long):**
Your dating apps aren't broken. Your dating frame is.

Here's what I mean by that.

When you date from the wound of your divorce, you unconsciously seek out women who will validate that wound. Your nervous system is literally scanning for familiar patterns — even if those patterns destroyed your marriage.

It's like this: Imagine you're shopping for a house while you're still angry about the last house you lived in. You'll either avoid houses that remind you of that one (and miss great opportunities) or you'll be drawn to houses with the exact same problems because they feel "familiar."

That's what happens when you date from your wound instead of from wholeness.

The woman who ghosts you? She reminds you of your ex's emotional unavailability.
The woman who comes on too strong? She triggers your fear of being controlled again.
The woman who seems perfect but "something feels off"? Your wound is rejecting someone who might actually be healthy.

You're not broken. You're just dating from the wrong operating system.

After my own divorce, I spent 18 months proving this theory wrong. Made $180K as a therapist but couldn't figure out why every woman I dated felt like a rerun.

Until I learned to date from wholeness instead of from my wound.

That's when everything changed.

**Headline:** [No headline needed for content consumption ad]

**Description:** [No description needed]

**CTA Button:** [No CTA button - optimizing for engagement]

**Suggested Image/Video Direction:** Simple graphic showing two paths diverging - one labeled "Dating from Wound" and one "Dating from Wholeness"

**Targeting Notes:**
- Audience: Retargeting (engaged with previous ads/content)
- Optimization: Post engagement
- Budget: [COACH: Insert your real dollar amount here]-30/day

---

## Ad 5: Direct Offer (Warm Audience Only)

**Primary Text (Short):**
Ready to become The Emotionally Intelligent Second-Act Man? The Second Act Method starts next month.

**Primary Text (Long):**
Ready to become The Emotionally Intelligent Second-Act Man? The Second Act Method starts next month.

If you're a divorced man over 45 who's been single for 2-4 years with no real traction on dating apps, this is for you.

Not if you're looking for pickup lines or dating hacks. Not if you want to date women half your age. Not if you think the problem is just finding better matches.

This is for men who are ready to stop dating from the wound of their divorce and start choosing from wholeness.

The Second Act Method is a 6-month, 3-phase program based on Gottman Institute attachment frameworks:

**Phase 1 - Grounding:** 4 weeks of post-divorce identity work to reset your emotional baseline
**Phase 2 - Rebuilding:** 8 weeks of attachment pattern work to establish a new dating frame
**Phase 3 - Attracting:** 12 weeks of real-world dating practice with right-fit women

You get weekly group coaching calls, monthly 1-on-1s, access to our 3000-person Brotherhood community, quarterly retreats, profile audits, and text review.

Investment: $9500 for 6 months.

Guarantee: If you don't have three meaningful dates per month by month 4, you get a 12-month extension.

420 men have been through this program. 63 out of 80 recent graduates are now in committed relationships.

This isn't therapy. This isn't pickup artistry. This is a systematic approach to becoming the man who attracts the right woman because he finally knows who he is.

If that's you, apply now. We start next month and cap enrollment at 40 men.

**Headline:** The Second Act Method - Next Cohort

**Description:** 6 months to find your person

**CTA Button:** Apply Now

**Suggested Image/Video Direction:** Professional photo of David speaking to a group of men, with program details overlay

**Targeting Notes:**
- Audience: Warm only (website visitors, lead magnet downloaders)
- Optimization: Conversions (applications)
- Budget: $40-75/day

relationship-dense.content:
# YouTube Video Script: "Why Divorced Men Over 45 Keep Dating the Same Woman (And How to Break the Cycle)"

**Video Summary:**
- **Title:** Why Divorced Men Over 45 Keep Dating the Same Woman (And How to Break the Cycle)
- **Thumbnail Text:** "SAME WOMAN, DIFFERENT FACE"
- **Core Belief Shift:** From "I have bad luck with women" → "I'm dating from my divorce wound instead of wholeness"
- **Target Length:** 12-15 minutes (~2,000 words)
- **Description:** Discover why successful divorced men keep attracting the same type of woman and the 3-phase system that breaks the pattern for good. If you're tired of first dates that go nowhere, this video reveals the real problem and the proven solution.

---

## 1. HOOK (0:00-0:30) [~75 words]

Listen, if you're a divorced man over 45 who keeps going on first dates that never turn into second dates, and you feel like you're invisible to quality women despite your success... this video is going to show you how to stop attracting women who remind you of your ex without changing your dating app photos, so you can find a partner who actually chooses you and feel confident in your selection instead of desperate for any connection.

[ON SCREEN: "Stop Dating Your Ex Over and Over"]

Right now, you probably think your problem is bad luck, broken dating apps, or low-quality women. But what I'm about to share with you is going to challenge everything you believe about why your dating life isn't working.

---

## 2. INTRO (0:30-1:30) [~150 words]

My name is David Alvarez. I've coached 420 divorced men over the past 9 years, built and sold a dating site for $1.2M, and I'm remarried to a woman I met in my own program community. I went from sleeping on a mattress on the floor after my divorce to becoming The Emotionally Intelligent Second-Act Man — and I've helped hundreds of other men do the same.

[ON SCREEN: "David Alvarez - 420 Men Coached"]

In this video, you're going to learn three things: First, the real reason you keep attracting the same type of woman. Second, why dating apps and generic advice will never solve your problem. And third, the exact 3-phase system that breaks the pattern for good.

Now, this video is for divorced men between 45-60 who make good money but can't get past first dates. If you're looking for pickup artist tricks or quick fixes, this isn't for you. But if you're ready to understand the psychology behind your dating patterns and do the work to change them, keep watching.

---

## 3. PROBLEM (1:30-4:00) [~375 words]

Here's what's really happening, and this might sting a little. You probably think your problem is that you need better matches, better photos, or a different dating app. You think if you could just get in front of the right women, everything would work out.

[ON SCREEN: "The Problem Isn't Your Photos"]

But here's the brutal truth: you're not looking for a woman. You're looking for proof that your ex was right about you.

Let me explain what I mean. After your divorce, you developed what I call wounded attachment patterns. Your nervous system learned that love equals pain, that vulnerability leads to betrayal, that women will eventually leave or hurt you. So unconsciously, you seek out women who validate these beliefs.

[PROOF BLOCK: Universal Analogy] It's like having a broken bone that healed wrong. You can put makeup on it, dress it up, make it look good from the outside, but it's still fundamentally broken. Dating apps and better strategies are just makeup on a broken bone.

Think about the last three women you dated. I bet they had similar patterns — maybe they were emotionally unavailable, or they reminded you of your ex's worst traits, or they triggered the same arguments you had in your marriage. That's not coincidence. That's your wounded attachment system seeking familiar pain.

[PROOF BLOCK: Personal Story] After my own divorce in 2014, I spent 18 months dating badly. I kept attracting women who were angry, distrustful, just like I was. I thought it was bad luck. Then my mentor told me something that broke me open: "You're not dating to find a partner. You're dating to prove your ex wrong. That's why every relationship fails."

[ON SCREEN: "Dating to Prove Your Ex Wrong"]

And that's when I realized — I was dating from the wound of my divorce, not from wholeness. Every swipe, every message, every first date was me trying to prove I wasn't the man my ex said I was. But wounded people attract wounded people. Always.

So if you're getting matches but no meaningful connections, if every woman seems to have the same red flags, if you feel invisible to quality women — the problem isn't out there. It's in here. [Point to chest]

---

## 4. NEW BELIEF / PARADIGM SHIFT (4:00-8:00) [~600 words]

Here's the truth that changes everything: The quality of women you attract is a direct reflection of your internal emotional state. If you're dating from the wound of your divorce, you'll keep attracting wounded women. But when you date from wholeness, you naturally attract women who are looking for what you're offering instead of what you're trying to escape.

[ON SCREEN: "Wound Attracts Wound, Wholeness Attracts Wholeness"]

[PROOF BLOCK: Authority Reference] Dr. John Gottman's research at the Gottman Institute shows that successful relationships depend on attachment security, not dating strategies. His decades of studying couples reveals that how you attach to partners is based on your internal emotional patterns, not external circumstances. You can have perfect photos and still repel quality women if your attachment system is wounded.

[PROOF BLOCK: Universal Analogy] Think about it like this: you wouldn't try to fix a leaky roof by buying better rain gear. You'd fix the roof itself. Dating apps and strategies are rain gear — they might keep you dry temporarily, but they don't fix the leak. Your attachment patterns from your divorce are the leak. Until you fix that, you'll keep getting soaked no matter how much gear you buy.

[PROOF BLOCK: Cross-Domain Reference] You already know this works in business. You wouldn't try to build a $200K+ income with just better business cards and networking events. You'd work on your skills, mindset, and value proposition first. The external strategies only work when your internal foundation is solid. You already apply this principle to make money — now apply it to find love.

[PROOF BLOCK: Client Story] Let me tell you about Carlos M. He was a restaurant owner, divorced 2 years, drinking too much and dating women 15 years younger. He thought his problem was finding age-appropriate women. But his real problem was that he was dating from the wound of his divorce — seeking validation instead of connection. After 6 months in The Second Act Method plus 1 retreat, he got sober and has been dating an age-appropriate partner for 9 months. As he told me: "I stopped running from myself."

[ON SCREEN: "Carlos M. - Sober & In Love"]

[PROOF BLOCK: Logic Trap] Here's something to think about: if better matches were really the solution, then successful men like you would have the easiest time dating. You have money, status, accomplishments. But you're struggling just like everyone else, which proves the problem isn't external. The problem is internal.

[PROOF BLOCK: Reframe] What if I told you that every woman who ghosts you is actually doing you a favor? She's showing you exactly what you're unconsciously communicating. Desperation, neediness, emotional unavailability — these things show up in subtle ways that quality women can sense immediately. They're not rejecting you because you're not good enough. They're protecting themselves from your unhealed wounds.

Now, I know what some of you are thinking: "David, dating coaches are for 25-year-olds." Listen, post-divorce dating at 45+ is emotional heart surgery. You wouldn't use a general contractor to perform heart surgery — you'd want a cardiac specialist who understands your specific condition. The Second Act Method isn't generic dating advice — it's specialized frameworks designed specifically for men rebuilding after marriage.

[PROOF BLOCK: Statistics] Out of my last 80 clients, 63 achieved meaningful relationship results by addressing their attachment wounds first instead of just optimizing their dating profiles. That's not luck — that's a system that works.

[ON SCREEN: "63 Out of 80 Men Found Love"]

The truth is, you don't have a dating app problem. You have an attachment wound problem. And until you heal that wound, you'll keep attracting the same woman with different faces.

---

## 5. PROOF / STORIES (8:00-12:00) [~600 words]

Let me tell you exactly how I discovered this. After my divorce in 2014, I was 38 years old, making $180K in my private therapy practice, but I was sleeping on a mattress on the floor of a rental. For 18 months, I tried everything — every dating app, read every book, went to therapy. But no matter what I did, I kept attracting the same type of woman.

[ON SCREEN: "18 Months of Dating Badly"]

They were angry like I was. Distrustful like I was. They reminded me of my ex's worst traits. I'd get triggered on dates, become defensive, start the same arguments I had in my marriage. I thought it was just bad luck with women.

Then one day, my mentor said something that shattered my entire worldview: "You're not dating to find a partner. You're dating to prove your ex wrong. That's why every relationship fails."

[ON SCREEN: "You're Dating to Prove Your Ex Wrong"]

And in that moment, I realized I was unconsciously seeking women who would validate my negative beliefs about relationships. I was dating from wound, not wholeness. So I did something radical — I stopped dating entirely and spent 2 years doing my own therapy work.

I closed my private practice and launched a dating program specifically for men coming out of divorce. I developed what I now call The Second Act Method — a 3-phase system that addresses the root cause instead of just treating symptoms.

Today, I have a 3,000-person community, I run 6 retreats per year, and I'm remarried to a woman I met in my own program community. But more importantly, I've helped 420 men make the same transformation.

[ON SCREEN: "420 Men Transformed"]

Like Dr. James K. He was a cardiac surgeon who had been divorced 3 years with 0 second dates in 18 months. Zero. This is a man who saves lives for a living, but he couldn't get a woman to have coffee with him twice.

He had tried every dating app, paid for premium features, even hired a photographer for better profile pics. But he was still The Wounded-Heart Repeater — dating from his divorce wound instead of his wholeness.

[ON SCREEN: "Dr. James K. - 0 Second Dates in 18 Months"]

After 6 months in The Second Act Method, he became The Emotionally Intelligent Second-Act Man. He got engaged to a woman he met at our November retreat. Eight months from hopeless to engaged. As he told me: "The only reason I'm not alone at 52 is this program."

[ON SCREEN: "Engaged in 8 Months"]

Or Mike D., a tech founder who avoided dating entirely for 18 months because he was so afraid of getting hurt again. He thought he could figure it out himself when he was "ready." But ready never came. After doing the full program plus 2 retreats, he's in a committed relationship with someone he calls "the right person at the right time." As he said: "I wasn't broken. I was healing."

Here's what's happening in the broader world that proves this principle. Harvard's Grant Study, which followed men for over [COACH: Insert your real timeframe here], found that the quality of relationships is the strongest predictor of happiness and life satisfaction — stronger than money, career success, or health. 

[ON SCREEN: "Harvard Grant Study - [COACH: Insert your real timeframe here]"]

But here's the key: the study also showed that men who never processed their emotional wounds from early relationships continued to struggle in all future relationships. The pattern doesn't break itself. It requires intentional intervention.

That's why The Second Act Method works. It's not about getting more matches. It's about healing the wound so you naturally attract wholeness instead of brokenness.

---

## 6. ACTION STEPS (12:00-15:00) [~450 words]

Alright, so what can you do today to start breaking this pattern? I'm going to give you three specific steps you can take right now to begin dating from wholeness instead of wound.

[ON SCREEN: "3 Steps to Break the Pattern"]

**Step 1: Take the Emotional Inventory**

Tonight, I want you to write down the last three women you dated or were attracted to. For each one, write down what attracted you to her and what red flags you ignored. Look for patterns. Did they all have similar issues with trust? Were they emotionally unavailable? Did they remind you of your ex in specific ways?

This isn't about judging them or yourself. It's about recognizing your unconscious selection criteria. Most divorced men are shocked when they see the pattern in black and white.

**Step 2: Identify Your Triggers**

For the next week, I want you to track your emotional triggers. When do you feel that familiar anger, that defensive reaction, that urge to prove yourself? Maybe it's when a woman takes too long to text back, or when she mentions her ex, or when she seems too independent.

Write down the trigger, the emotion it brings up, and what story you tell yourself about what it means. This is how you start recognizing when you're operating from your wound instead of your wisdom.

[ON SCREEN: "Track Your Triggers Daily"]

**Step 3: Practice the Pause**

Before you swipe, before you send that message, before you react to something she said — pause. Ask yourself: "Am I responding from my wound or my wholeness right now?" If you're feeling desperate, needy, angry, or defensive — that's your wound talking. Take a breath, center yourself, and choose a response that comes from your best self.

This simple practice will immediately improve the quality of your interactions because you'll stop leading with your pain and start leading with your presence.

[ON SCREEN: "Pause Before You React"]

Now, these three steps will give you immediate insight into your patterns, but they're just the beginning. If you're serious about breaking the cycle completely, you need a comprehensive system that addresses the root cause.

That's what The Second Act Method does. Phase 1 resets your emotional baseline through grounding work. Phase 2 rebuilds your attachment patterns using proven frameworks. Phase 3 helps you attract the right women through real-world practice.

But here's what I want you to understand: you can't think your way through an emotional problem. You have to feel your way through the healing. And that requires support, accountability, and expert guidance.

[ON SCREEN: "Healing Requires Support"]

If you're tired of attracting the same woman with different faces, if you're ready to become The Emotionally Intelligent Second-Act Man who chooses from wholeness instead of settling from loneliness, then it's time to stop trying to figure this out alone.

---

## 7. CTA (15:00-End) [~225 words]

So remember — it's not about finding better women. It's about becoming a better version of yourself. It's not about getting more matches. It's about healing the attachment wound that keeps you stuck in the same pattern.

[ON SCREEN: "Heal the Wound, Attract Wholeness"]

When you do this work, everything changes. You wake up knowing exactly who you are and what you bring to a relationship. You attract women who tell you you're different from other men they've dated. Your kids see you as a man who rebuilt himself. You sleep peacefully knowing you're on the path to lasting love.

If you're ready to take the next step, I've created a free resource called The Second-Act Dating Audit. It's a comprehensive assessment that will show you exactly where you're dating from wound versus wholeness, and give you a personalized roadmap for transformation.

[ON SCREEN: "Get The Second-Act Dating Audit - Free"]

The link is in the description below. And if you're ready to go deeper, I also offer discovery calls where we can talk about whether The Second Act Method is right for your situation.

If this video helped you see your dating patterns in a new way, hit the subscribe button and ring the bell — I post new videos every week for divorced men who are serious about finding lasting love.

Drop a comment below and tell me: what pattern did you recognize in yourself? I read every comment and I'll respond personally.

Remember, you deserve to be chosen, not settled for. The right woman is out there, but first, you need to become the right man for her.

[ON SCREEN: "You Deserve to Be Chosen"]

---

**Estimated Runtime: 13-14 minutes at 150 words/minute**

**Headline Text:** "1. Treating Dating Like Business"
**Body Text:** You think logic and strategy will solve emotional problems. Dating requires vulnerability, not spreadsheets.
**Visual Direction:** Business charts crossed out, heart vs. brain imagery, logic vs. emotion contrast

### Slide 3
**Headline Text:** "2. Optimizing the Wrong Things"
**Body Text:** Better photos won't fix attachment wounds. You're polishing the outside while the inside is still broken from divorce.
**Visual Direction:** Camera/photo imagery crossed out, surface vs. depth representation, optimization symbols

### Slide 4
**Headline Text:** "3. Dating Too Soon"
**Body Text:** You think you're ready because you want a relationship. But wanting and being emotionally ready are completely different things.
**Visual Direction:** Calendar/timing imagery, readiness vs. desire contrast, emotional preparation symbols

### Slide 5
**Headline Text:** "4. Avoiding Expert Help"
**Body Text:** You figured out business alone, so you think you can figure out post-divorce dating alone. Different skills, different game.
**Visual Direction:** Solo vs. team imagery, expert guidance symbols, different skill set representation

### Slide 6
**Headline Text:** "5. Settling or Avoiding"
**Body Text:** You either date anyone who'll have you or avoid dating entirely. Both strategies come from fear, not confidence.
**Visual Direction:** Extremes imagery, fear vs. confidence contrast, balanced approach representation

### Slide 7
**Headline Text:** "The Solution"
**Body Text:** Address your attachment wounds first. Date from wholeness, not wound. Get expert guidance for faster results.
**Visual Direction:** Solution/success styling, healing imagery, expert guidance symbols, positive colors

**Caption:**
Listen, I see these mistakes constantly with the successful men who find me.

You've built a $200K+ income through logic, strategy, and hard work. So you think dating should work the same way.

It doesn't.

Post-divorce dating is emotional surgery, not business development. You need different skills, different frameworks, and different support.

I made every single one of these mistakes after my divorce. Spent 18 months optimizing photos and profiles while my attachment wounds sabotaged every relationship before it started.

The moment I stopped treating dating like a business problem and started treating it like an emotional healing opportunity, everything changed.

**Comment MISTAKES and I'll send you the attachment wound assessment to see which of these mistakes you're making.**

#DatingMistakes #SuccessfulMen #PostDivorce #AttachmentHealing #MenOver45 #DatingStrategy #EmotionalIntelligence #RelationshipSuccess

**ManyChat Trigger:** MISTAKES → Sends attachment wound assessment and mistake prevention guide

relationship-dense.content:
# Short-Form Video Scripts for David Alvarez

---

## Short #1 — Hook Clip
**Type:** Pattern Interrupt / Scroll Stopper
**Platform:** Instagram Reels, TikTok, YouTube Shorts
**Duration:** 30 seconds

### Hook
"You're not dating to find a partner. You're dating to prove your ex wrong."

### Body Script
You're not dating to find a partner. You're dating to prove your ex wrong. That's why every relationship fails.

Listen, after my divorce in 2014, I spent 18 months attracting the same type of woman over and over. Angry, distrustful, just like I was. I thought it was bad luck.

Then my mentor broke it down: I wasn't looking for love. I was looking for validation that my ex was wrong about me. But wounded people attract wounded people. Always.

The quality of women you attract is a direct reflection of your internal emotional state. If you're dating from the wound of your divorce, you'll keep attracting wounded women.

Want to break the cycle? Stop trying to prove anything to anyone and start healing the wound.

### On-Screen Text
- "Dating to prove your ex wrong"
- "Wounded attracts wounded"
- "Heal the wound first"

### CTA/Closing
"Follow for more truth about post-divorce dating."

### Suggested Visual
Talking head with serious expression, cut to black screen with white text for key phrases

### Hashtags
#DivorcedDad #DatingOver45 #PostDivorce #MensDating #SecondChances

---

## Short #2 — Belief Shift Clip
**Type:** Core Belief Challenge
**Platform:** Instagram Reels, TikTok, YouTube Shorts
**Duration:** 45 seconds

### Hook
"Your dating problem isn't bad luck. It's broken attachment."

### Body Script
Your dating problem isn't bad luck. It's broken attachment.

You think you need better matches, better photos, a different dating app. But here's the brutal truth: you're unconsciously seeking women who validate your negative beliefs about relationships.

Think about the last three women you dated. I bet they had similar patterns. Emotionally unavailable. Reminded you of your ex's worst traits. Triggered the same arguments you had in your marriage.

That's not coincidence. That's your wounded attachment system seeking familiar pain.

It's like having a broken bone that healed wrong. You can put makeup on it, dress it up, make it look good from the outside, but it's still fundamentally broken.

Dating apps are just makeup on a broken bone. Fix the bone first.

### On-Screen Text
- "Not bad luck = broken attachment"
- "Seeking familiar pain"
- "Fix the bone, not the makeup"

### CTA/Closing
"Ready to fix the real problem? Link in bio for my free dating audit."

### Suggested Visual
Split screen showing dating app swipes vs. internal emotional work, transition to talking head

### Hashtags
#AttachmentTheory #DatingTips #DivorcedMen #EmotionalHealing #DatingCoach

---

## Short #3 — Hook Clip
**Type:** Contrarian Take / Authority Challenge
**Platform:** Instagram Reels, TikTok, YouTube Shorts
**Duration:** 35 seconds

### Hook
"Stop optimizing your dating profile. Start optimizing your attachment system."

### Body Script
Stop optimizing your dating profile. Start optimizing your attachment system.

You're a successful man. You make good money. You have your life together. But you can't get past first dates. Why?

Because quality women can sense your unhealed wounds immediately. They're not rejecting you because you're not good enough. They're protecting themselves from your emotional unavailability.

Every woman who ghosts you is actually doing you a favor. She's showing you exactly what you're unconsciously communicating.

You wouldn't try to fix a leaky roof by buying better rain gear. You'd fix the roof itself. Your attachment patterns are the leak.

### On-Screen Text
- "Stop optimizing profiles"
- "Women sense unhealed wounds"
- "Fix the leak, not the symptoms"

### CTA/Closing
"Comment 'LEAK' if this hit different."

### Suggested Visual
Mock dating profile optimization vs. internal work imagery, serious talking head delivery

### Hashtags
#DatingReality #MenOver45 #PostDivorce #DatingMindset #AttachmentHealing

---

## Short #4 — Story Clip
**Type:** Personal Transformation
**Platform:** Instagram Reels, TikTok, YouTube Shorts
**Duration:** 50 seconds

### Hook
"I was making $180K and sleeping on a mattress on the floor."

### Body Script
I was making $180K and sleeping on a mattress on the floor.

After my divorce in 2014, I had my career together but my personal life was a disaster. For 18 months, I kept attracting the same type of woman. They were angry like I was. Distrustful like I was.

I tried every dating app, read every book, went to therapy. Nothing worked.

Then my mentor said something that broke me open: "You're not dating to find a partner. You're dating to prove your ex wrong."

I realized I was dating from wound, not wholeness. So I stopped dating entirely and spent 2 years healing.

Today I've coached 420 men, I'm remarried to a woman I met in my own program community, and I run a 3000-person brotherhood.

The work changed everything.

### On-Screen Text
- "$180K but sleeping on the floor"
- "Dating from wound vs wholeness"
- "420 men coached, remarried"

### CTA/Closing
"Your transformation starts with one decision. Are you ready?"

### Suggested Visual
Before/after lifestyle shots, transition to confident talking head, end with community footage

### Hashtags
#TransformationTuesday #DivorcedDadWins #SecondActSuccess #PostDivorce #MensCoaching

---

## Short #5 — Belief Shift Clip
**Type:** Reframe Challenge
**Platform:** Instagram Reels, TikTok, YouTube Shorts
**Duration:** 40 seconds

### Hook
"You don't have a dating app problem. You have an attachment wound problem."

### Body Script
You don't have a dating app problem. You have an attachment wound problem.

If better matches were really the solution, then successful men like you would have the easiest time dating. You have money, status, accomplishments. But you're struggling just like everyone else.

That proves the problem isn't external. The problem is internal.

Your nervous system learned that love equals pain, that vulnerability leads to betrayal, that women will eventually leave. So unconsciously, you seek out women who validate these beliefs.

Until you heal that wound, you'll keep attracting the same woman with different faces.

The Emotionally Intelligent Second-Act Man doesn't need more matches. He needs more wholeness.

### On-Screen Text
- "Not a dating app problem"
- "Attachment wound problem"
- "Same woman, different faces"
- "More wholeness, not matches"

### CTA/Closing
"Ready to heal the wound? Follow for the roadmap."

### Suggested Visual
Dating app interface dissolving into internal emotional work graphics, confident talking head close-up

### Hashtags
#AttachmentWounds #DatingMindset #EmotionalIntelligence #MenOver45 #HealingJourney

---

## Short #6 — CTA Clip
**Type:** Direct Response / Lead Generation
**Platform:** Instagram Reels, TikTok, YouTube Shorts
**Duration:** 35 seconds

### Hook
"Are you The Wounded-Heart Repeater or The Emotionally Intelligent Second-Act Man?"

### Body Script
Are you The Wounded-Heart Repeater or The Emotionally Intelligent Second-Act Man?

The Wounded-Heart Repeater dates from his divorce wound. He attracts women who remind him of his ex. He gets triggered on dates. He feels invisible to quality women.

The Emotionally Intelligent Second-Act Man dates from wholeness. He attracts women who choose him, not settle for him. He's selective, confident, at peace with his history.

I've created a free assessment called The Second-Act Dating Audit that shows you exactly where you're dating from wound versus wholeness.

This is only for divorced men over 45 who make good money but can't get past first dates.

### On-Screen Text
- "Wounded-Heart vs Second-Act Man"
- "Dating from wound vs wholeness"
- "Free Second-Act Dating Audit"
- "Divorced men 45+ only"

### CTA/Closing
"Link in bio. Take the audit. Find out which man you are."

### Suggested Visual
Split screen showing two different men, transition to assessment preview, end with direct eye contact

### Hashtags
#SecondActDating #DatingAudit #MenOver45 #PostDivorce #FreeAssessment

---

## Short #7 — Hook Clip
**Type:** Statistic Shock / Authority
**Platform:** Instagram Reels, TikTok, YouTube Shorts
**Duration:** 30 seconds

### Hook
"Out of my last 80 clients, 63 found meaningful relationships. Here's how."

### Body Script
Out of my last 80 clients, 63 found meaningful relationships. Here's how.

They stopped trying to fix their dating profiles and started fixing their attachment wounds.

Dr. James K was a cardiac surgeon with 0 second dates in 18 months. After 6 months in The Second Act Method, he got engaged to a woman he met at our retreat.

Carlos M was drinking too much and dating women 15 years younger. Now he's sober and in love with an age-appropriate partner for 9 months.

The pattern is clear: heal the wound first, find love second.

### On-Screen Text
- "63 out of 80 found love"
- "Fix attachment wounds first"
- "Dr. James: 0 dates → engaged"
- "Carlos: sober & in love"

### CTA/Closing
"Want to be number 64? Follow for the method."

### Suggested Visual
Statistics overlay, client success photos (with permission), confident talking head delivery

### Hashtags
#ClientResults #DatingSuccess #SecondActMethod #PostDivorce #ProvenSystem
